memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with low energy and high sleep debt. The term project is due Thursday, but I only barely started it.
Working 2 hours advanced the project to 28%, but I still feel behind. Procrastinating is increasing my future stress despite my low energy.
After two hours, my progress jumped to 47%, significantly reducing my stress. I now feel more confident about meeting the Thursday deadline despite remaining sleep debt.
After 2 hours, my project progress jumped to 61%, significantly reducing stress despite 2 hours of sleep debt.
Progress jumped from 61% to 70% in two hours, significantly reducing stress. I feel more confident about the Thursday deadline despite my sleep debt.
While my major project progress increased to 78%, I failed the instant win trivial chore. My energy is critically low, and sleep debt remains high.
Progress improved from 0.78 to 0.86 in two hours, confirming my confidence despite high stress and sleep debt.
After sleeping only 4 hours, my energy is high but stress remains critically elevated despite recent progress gains.
My term project is complete, but my previous failure and low sleep leave me with high stress despite feeling energetic.
A trivial task yielded minimal progress (0.03), confirming my earlier observation that high stress persists despite recent gains and current energy levels.
The term project is finished, yet stress remains critically high despite zero sleep debt and full energy.
The term project is done, yet a trivial task only yielded minimal progress while stress remains critically high despite full energy and no sleep debt.
